questions.rule_description: Vaguely self-similar (looks like self-similar fractal after one iteration)
The collection of dot clumps has the same numerical property as each of the dot clumps
The sum of all dot clumps has the same numerical property as each of the dot clumps
Polygon can be inscribed in a circle
Shapes become thicker the farther they are from the top-left and bottom-right corners of bounding box
some other pattern in shape thickness based on position.
Segment drawn between two points intersects another line
segment drawn between two points does not intersect any other lines
Line segments linking same-coloured dots would intersect
White space in two circles maximum
white space in three circles maximum.
Solution is "one dot vs. more than one dot" vs. other solution.
"Specific number vs. other number" vs. other solution.
"Less than ___ vs. greater than ___" vs.  other solution.
"Multiple of ___ vs. other number" vs. other solution.
A multiple of a left number fits right
"Greater than ___ vs. less than ___" vs. other solution.
One circle passes through the center of the other circle
no circle passes through the center of the other circle.
Left is union of (non-constant) arithmetic progressions
Ends of line point to the same direction
ends of line do not point to the same direction.
Two parts black to one part white
All nonempty boxes have same number of boxes in them
Every nonempty region has one box
Each large shape is made up of small shapes which are the same shape as the other large shape
Some region has one box in it
There is an infinite one-one-one-one-... chain present
Every nonempty region has two boxes
Filled subsection is largest square that divides the grid
All regions have the same number of boxes
Image of Bongard Problem where each left example corresponds intuitively to a right example
image of Bongard Problem whose examples do not share this correlation.
Endpoints of curve have the same height
A vertical line may pass through both objects
Top piece can fit into bottom piece without leaving any overhangs
Circle on the right of the box
circle on the left of the box.
Top piece can slide into bottom area in such that there is an unbroken black horizontal region
Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection
Four out of five objects are circles
never four out of five objects are circles.
There is a path between any two nodes
Nodes share the same edge connections as the vertices of a cube
Maze object features multiple branching paths
Contains smaller copy of self with black and white inverted
middle shape is not a triangle.
There is no rule for how the objects in a cluster interrelate
Circle that passes through points is contained within bounding box
Each attribute is shared by every group or none
some attribute is shared by exactly two groups
Columns of the table could be respectively labeled "Number" and "Number of times number appears in this table"
Number of dots in the Nth box (from the left) is how many times the number (N - 1) appears in the whole diagram
Number in the Nth box (from the left) is how many numbers appear N times
Innermost shape is reachable from the outermost one
innermost shape is unreachable from the outermost one.
Section of the image is a Bongard Problem
no section of the image is a Bongard Problem.
Shapes are sorted according to a simple rule that uniquely determines where everything goes
shapes are sorted according to some other rule (or lack thereof).
The order in which the objects in the top half are combined to make the object in the lower half matters
Image contains the exact arrangement of pixels that form the "S" creature depicted inEX9532in exactly one place
arrangement is present in multiple places.
Triangle points to center of circle
triangle does not point to center of circle.
Each symbol appears once in any given row or column
A small correction will result in a single circle
no small correction will result in a single circle.
No sequence is repeated twice in a row
some sequence is repeated twice in a row.
All turns are in one direction and rectangular loop exists
turns in different directions and no rectangular loop exists.
The only triangles are the individual regions and the whole
there is some other triangle made of triangles.
Objects below match their "symbols" above, according to one "code" versus objects below match their "symbols" above, according to another "code."
Line divides interior into two regions
curves only cross themselves (or do not cross at all).
When two players alternate coloring regions, either can force connection from top edge to bottom edge
either can force connection from left edge to right edge.
Any symmetry exhibited by some non-empty subset of the objects is also a symmetry of the whole thing
No straight path from one side of the panel to the other passes through the grey shape without hitting a black region
No straight path passes from a white region to a grey one, then to white again (without hitting black)
Any straight path will hit a black region
One protrusion and one indentation of the same shape
Arrows which if moved at speeds proportional to their length would pass eachother
The rule is about squares having a certain relationship with their neighbors
One large and one small object
Same transformation applied to circle, triangle, and square
Angles either only increase or only decrease as one moves along the curve
Any two lines intersect, and no three lines share an intersection point
Identical pairs of symbols are always on opposite sides of the cube (when assembled)
some identical symbols are next to each other on the cube.
Straight line can separate the two types of figures
Sequence contains each possible way its distinct elements can be arranged as a subsequence
Ways of representing the sequence "ABABCBACCBAC" by grouping its elements into equal-sized blocks and relabelling them (identical blocks are represented by the same element)
There is a way of dividing the grid into (more than one) equal-sized blocks such that no block appears more than once
there exists no such way of dividing the grid.
Same objects inside and outside the large shape
not same objects inside and outside the large shape.
Circled points are all possible vertices a regular hexagon with a particular side length can take, provided that each of its corners lie on a grid point
Circled points are all possible vertices an equilateral triangle with a particular side length can take, provided that each of its corners lie on a grid point
If two players take turns moving moving the black circles with the intention of capturing their opponent's piece, one can always "checkmate" the other
the game results in a draw if the players play optimally.
Black points are the set of vertices that are some shortest-distance away from some white vertex
Closed region is made of curves if flaws are ignored
closed region is a polygon if flaws are ignored.
Dots are vertices of a parallelogram
dots are not vertices of a parallelogram.
Dots are vertices of a triangle standing on a side
dots are vertices of a triangle standing on a vertex.
Dots collinear with center of circle
dots not collinear with center of circle.
Circles on same side of curve
circles on different sides of curves.
Dots equal in number to the sides of the closed region
dots unequal in number to the sides of the closed region.
No dot well within the convex hull
at least one dot well within the convex hull.
One large shape and two smaller identical ones
Two clusters of three and two objects each
one cluster of three objects and two different objects.
Two clusters of three and two that are adjacent
two clusters of three and two that are not adjacent.
Two clusters of three and two
two clusters of four and one.
Two clusters of three and two: different features (including clustering) yield different 3-2 splits
two clusters of three and two: different features yield the same 3-2 split.
Different ways of grouping yield same 4-1 split
different ways of grouping yield different 4-1 splits.
A shape contains a square if and only if it is a circle
a shape contains a square if and only if it is a triangle.
Three collinear and two others, the two are vertical
A little less than a regular shape
a little more than a regular shape.
If the circle closest to the cross is removed, the rest form an equilateral triangle
Predominance of curves and hook-like endings
predominance of straight lines and staple-like endings.
Curves are longer than straight lines
curves are shorter than straight lines.
Two clusters of 2+3, middle shape must be seen as rectangle (AND only one linelike shape)
two clusters of 2+3, middle shape must be seen as piece of straight line (AND more than one linelike shape).
Quadrilateral that is nearly a triangle
Every other side, if extended, passes through one point
Line connecting small shapes does not intersect large one
line connecting small shapes intersects large one.
Number of objects is one less than sides of each object
number of objects is one more than sides of each object.
Line perpendicular to one side of the object
line parallel to one side of the object.
Every cluster has two clusters of dots
every cluster has three clusters of dots.
Two clusters of different numbers of elements
two clusters of equal number of elements.
Angle bisectors meet at the incenter
perpendicular bisectors meet at the orthocenter.
Small object can glide in the bay
small object cannot glide in the bay.
Line that connects dots avoiding obstacles is short
line that connects dots avoiding obstacles is long.
Center of circle in triangle perpendicular to the other two
"Pinched" shape (drastically thinner somewhere in the middle than on the ends)
Black region narrows at the boundary of black/white
black region widens at the boundary of black/white.
Concave if proximal border points are connected
convex if proximal border points are connected.
Same curvature close to the middle
change of curvature close to the middle.
Curve is smooth close to the middle
curve zigzags close to the middle.
object made of objects made of objects.
Sides of parts are one more than sides of whole
sides of parts are one less than sides of whole.
Shape of whole different from shape of parts
shape of whole same as shape of parts.
All clusters are made of objects of the same texture
not all clusters are made of objects of the same texture.
All connected objects have the same texture
some connected objects have different textures.
Bottom object in front of top objects in 3-D
bottom object behind top objects in 3-D.
Bongard Problem with solution based on features
Bongard Problem with solution based on number.
Two of the shapes make tiles along their border lines
Two clusters of two elements each
Curve ends in a roughly up or down direction
curve ends in a roughly left or right direction.
Curve ends on the left part of the box
curve ends on the right part of the box.
All curves spiral counterclockwise, starting from maximum curvature
all curves spiral clockwise, starting from maximum curvature.
No objects are centered at the center of the box
one object is centered at the center of the box.
No dot is too far apart from another dot
at least one dot is far apart from all other dots.
The tightest-curved section, out of all sections of curve that make a complete turn (360 degrees), contains an x-crossing point
an extra copy of something is added.
All shapes approximately the same size
No small square at the end of any curve
curve ends at a small square.
Border of black rectangle is incomplete
border of black rectangle is complete.
Large black region if completed is a circle
large black region if completed is an ellipse.
Diametrically opposite objects affect similarly the large black circular region
high contrast (at least one fully black region).
What comes out of black circle has a clockwise direction
Dots touch each other if and only if no large region touches the edges of the box
dots dont touch each other if and only if no large region touches the edges of the box.
After rotating so that the longest line is horizontal, there is a left arm raised and a weight down
after rotating so that the longest line is horizontal, there is a left arm down and a weight raised.
When the objects are rotated and their dots are overlapped and eliminated, they form three-square-tall structures with columns of the same color
The sum of vertices, line ends, and dots is 7
Circle falls by gravity on the right of all other objects if let loose
circle falls by gravity on the left of all other objects if let loose.
The rope, if extended, reaches the other ball
the rope, if extended, does not reach the other ball.
Upper central line APPEARS shorter than lower central line
upper central line APPEARS longer than lower central line.
C-like curves point to the same direction
c-like curves point to opposite directions.
Straight line parallel to line joining the ends of curve AND sliding the straight line suitably can make a closed area
straight line perpendicular to line joining the ends of curve.
Straight line joining the curve ends does not cut the curve
straight line joining the curve ends intersects the curve.
Circumference shorter than length of everything else
circumference longer than length of everything else.
convex hull is not a square.
Triangle closer to box corner than any of the circles
Scanning left-to-right, top-to-bottom, each filled box is separated from the next filled box by the same number of empty boxes
Can be completed to be made into a rectangle
cannot be completed to be made into a rectangle.
Equal numbers of straight lines and continuous curves
unequal numbers of straight lines and continuous curves.
not five straight or curved lines.
Dashed lines inside solid shape make an identical but slightly smaller shape, parallel to the one outside
Dashed lines form the same shape centered inside
Tetrahedron projected on the box plane
Tetrahedron projected on the box plane
Circle and two vertices are collinear
no two vertices and the circle are collinear.
Three pairs of parallel and non-collinear pieces of straight lines, each pair with a different slope
Each large circle closer to a small circle than a large one, and each small circle closer to a large one than a small one
The distance from the center of the line to the bottom of the square is the same as the distance from the dot to the right edge of the square
Two identical overlapping objects of which the top is rotated slightly clockwise
Squares are either all empty or all contain something
Straight lines that join filled and outlined circles intersect
straight lines that join filled and outlined circles do not intersect.
Exactly one point where lines bend at an angle
not exactly one point where lines bend at an angle.
At least one ending edge is straight
At least one touch or cross point
Odd number of straight line segments
even number of straight line segments.
Can be hand-written without raising the pen
cannot be hand-written without raising the pen.
Stays put (both when placed on its top and bottom)
no notch from above (does not hold poured water).
Straight line joining the ends does not intersect the object
straight line joining the ends intersects the object.
Shorter on the left, taller on the right
taller on the left, shorter on the right.
The left part has more pixels (area) than the right part
the left part has fewer pixels than the right part.
The left part is wider than the right part
the left part is narrower than the right part.
Equal number of horizontal and vertical lines
unequal number of horizontal and vertical lines.
Shape of black region has as many sides as overall object
shape of black region has different number of sides than overall object.
Rectangle left after tiling all black shapes into empty square
triangle left after tiling all black shapes into empty square.
The centers (barycenters) of the objects are collinear
the centers (barycenters) of the objects are not collinear.
Decreasing in size from top to bottom
increasing in size from top to bottom.
Rays emanating from a central point
The sum of the ratios of the filled areas is 1
the sum of the ratios of the filled areas is other than 1.
Shapes, if tiled up properly, form a square
shapes cannot form a square no matter how they are tiled.
There are more small circles inside the figure outline than outside
there are fewer small circles inside the figure outline than outside.
If any non-nosed faces exist, they are happy if and only if happy nosed faces exist as well
The shapes in the large square never appear outside of it
at least one shape in the large square appears outside of it.
Angle made by shapes with vertex at square is not acute
angle made by shapes with vertex at square is acute.
Square divided into four areas of the same size and shape
The two points are reachable through a path
the two points are unreachable by any path.
Line joins large and small square
line joins the two middle-sized squares.
only two of the three sizes present.
One of the two small squares is near the lower-right corner
one of the two small squares is near the upper-right corner.
Lines if slightly extended make up a regular polygon
lines if slightly extended cannot make up a regular polygon.
5 collinear objects, of which the 3rd is moved elsewhere
5 collinear objects, of which the 2nd/4th is moved elsewhere.
Three objects are identical, the fourth differs
At least one (interior or exterior) right angle
no right angle (either interior or exterior).
shapes identical after rotation and mirroring.
Ratio of inside to outside identical shapes is constant
Dots are in tight clusters around convex vertices
Three or more small clusters of dots
Ball touches curve at a point of local minimum downwards
ball touches curve at a point of local maximum upwards.
Enclosed square repelled by the outside squares, closest to the side of the enclosing square thats farthest from the common center of the outside squares.
enclosed square attracted by the outside squares, closest to the side of the enclosing square thats closest to the common center of the outside squares..
Number of sides minus number of pluses = 3
number of sides minus number of pluses = 4.
lines intersect out of the box.
Closed curves clockwise, open curves counterclockwise
closed curves counterclockwise, open curves clockwise.
Long distance between the marked locations
short distance between the marked locations.
One area unreachable by the marked locations
all areas reachable by the marked locations.
At least one path is a cycle

The numbers of dots can be put into a sequence of consecutive numbers
The number of dots in one cluster is a multiple of the other

the numbers of dots in the two clusters differ a lot.
Small round object unreachable from the border of the box
small round object reachable from the border of the box.
Jigsaw puzzle pieces can be assembled into a square
jigsaw puzzle pieces cannot be assembled into a square.
Left shapes can be placed on top of each other to make right shape

left shapes can combine by intersection (AND logical operator) to make right shape.
straight line not tangent to circle.
straight line not tangent to curve.
Union of left sets is right set
intersection of left sets is right set.
Strictly increasing or strictly decreasing border line
both increasing and decreasing border line.
either zero or more than one axis of symmetry.
No two shapes are the same
at least two shapes are the same.

object on the right does not fit in pattern on the left.

the axis of the hole is perpendicular to the figure axis.
Some quantity keeps increasing from left to right

increasing quantity cannot loop back to starting value.

the circles will not collide if the arrows are their velocities.
Choice that solves the analogy exists
choice that solves the analogy does not exist.
Counterclockwise along the curve following the arrow
clockwise along the curve following the arrow.

white dot is not at center of mass.

center of mass out of the black area of the shape.

there is no point that can see (in straight lines) all points.

shape below fits on the right.
A "chess piece" that moves as shown may reach every square

the two objects have dissimilar representations.
The two objects are conceptually related
the two objects are conceptually unrelated.
The completed version of the collection is finite
the completed version of the collection is infinite.
Adding the top two waves yields the bottom wave
When the shape is removed from the dots, the dots give enough information to place the shape back where it was
Lower shape can be used as a tile to build the upper one
Dots can be shared equally between figures
dots cannot be shared equally between figures.

white dot cannot see (in straight lines) all black points.
Segments approximately parallel to each other
Each graph vertex is uniquely defined by its connections (the graph does not admit nontrivial automorphisms)
There exists an edge such that removing it yields two disconnected graphs (i.e., the minimum number of edges whose removal results in two disconnected graphs is 1)
the minimum number of edges whose removal results in two disconnected graphs is 2.
For each colored square only, there exists a path starting on it that covers each square of the figure exactly once
there is no path that starts on a colored square and covers each square of the figure exactly once.
Three points on a straight line
no three points on a straight line.
Outline circles on one straight line
outline circles not on one straight line.
Points inside the figure outline are on a straight line
points inside the figure outline are not on a straight line.

the vibration amplitude decreases from left to right.

black figure on top of outline figure.

outline figures above the solid dark figures.

is smooth without straight lines or corners.

no two circles close to each other.

inside figure has more angles than outside figure.

a line does not separate the crosses in half.

extensions of segments do not cross at one point.

a dark circle is closer to the triangle than to the outline circle.

points located at different distances from a cross.

the convex hull of the crosses does not form an equilateral triangle.

a circle is outside of figures made by crosses.

a quadrangle is inside of a figure made by circles.

there is no object fitting between the sides.

right pattern is not subset of left pattern.

numbers of dots neither in ascending nor descending order from left to right.
